behavior_summary: This script provides the object's response when the player 'looks at' (examines) it: it emits a single short bark/text reading "table ".
The routine is implemented as a U8P_DTABLE::look-type process (processType 0x0208) so it is invoked by the engine's examine/U8P_DTABLE::look system.
There are no menu interactions, flag reads/writes, parameter usage, local variables, or external API calls present in the excerpt — the behavior is purely descriptive and immediate.
notable_control_flow: U8P_DTABLE::look executes a single player-facing Bark node with text "table ".
The bark is followed by a suspension/termination of the U8P_DTABLE::look process (raw tokens indicate a suspend/return after speaking).
No conditional branches, loops, or calls are present; control flow is linear and immediate.
open_questions: What is the significance of the trailing space in the bark text "table " — is it intentional formatting or an extraction artifact?
The source contained low-level tokens ('{' and 'suspend') around the bark; are these simply engine delimiters or do they encode additional lifecycle behavior?
Is this U8P_DTABLE::look handler ever extended by other scripts or external calls in other context (none are present here, but relationships outside this file are unknown)?

main_function.name: U8P_DTABLE::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player's examine/U8P_DTABLE::look action by displaying a short descriptive bark for the object.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game's U8P_DTABLE::look/examine system when the Avatar/player examines this object (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_DTABLE::look
function_map.role: Provides the object's short description when examined; emits a single bark and ends the U8P_DTABLE::look process.
